In return, the municipality of Purmerend will make sure it takes in the required number of Ukrainian refugees, while the smaller municipalities will house unaccompanied minor asylum seekers.
statements.pro: Accepting more than the legal requirement will be to the detriment of existing residents, as facilities will be under strain.More asylum seekers means more community disruption, some parties say.It is time for other municipalities to step up and do their part.
statements.con: It is socially-minded and compassionate for Zaanstad to take on more asylum seekers than the minimum.Accommodating asylum seekers does not cause any disruption, some parties say, so there is no reason to take on fewer.Some parties say that businesses are extremely happy with the employees from the asylum-seeker shelters.

statements.title: The municipality should combat the subdivision of houses into rented rooms.
statements.moreInfo: Property owners must apply to the municipality of Zaanstad if they wish to rent or sell a home separately as individual rooms. The municipality can decide whether to issue the permit, and under what conditions (only for houses of a certain size, for example).
statements.pro: Subdivision creates disruption for the surrounding residents.
statements.con: Subdivision creates additional housing, for young people and other groups.

statements.title: The municipality should allow solar panels on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality only has partial influence on whether solar panels can be installed on agricultural land. Provincial and national legislation also applies.
statements.pro: Sustainable energy production contributes to our climate targets.Solar panels will help reduce the power shortage.
statements.con: Solar panels on agricultural land damage the landscape.Agricultural land is intended for farmers to produce food.

statements.title: The municipality should fund a night train that runs between Zaanstad and Amsterdam.
statements.pro: A night train increases safety when travelling between Amsterdam and Zaanstad at night.A night train is relatively inexpensive, some parties say. There is already a night train between Amsterdam and Alkmaar, that only needs to stop in Zaanstad.
statements.con: There is no need for a night train, as there is already a night bus with many stops. Some parties believe it will be costly to have a night train stop in Zaanstad.

statements.title: The municipality should budget more for improved clubhouses and playing fields for sports associations.
statements.moreInfo: The ‘Participation in Sport’ (Meedoen in sport) scheme allows sports clubs to apply for funding from the municipality: to maintain or renovate their fields, for example.
statements.pro: Sport and exercise promote good health.
statements.con: Sports clubs need to be self-sufficient.

statements.title: Zaanstad should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focusing on safety.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for enforcement of minor infringements. These include parking tickets, incorrect bicycle parking, or littering. People who are caught are given a fine. The people who do this work are called ‘municipal enforcement officers,’ or BOAs in Dutch.  Police officers are for general enforcement, and have different duties and authorisations. There are also BOAs who do not work for the municipality, such as those on trains.
statements.pro: BOAs help create a safer city.Some parties say there are too few police officers on the streets. BOAs can help ensure that enforcement in Zaanstad remains adequate.
statements.con: Some parties say that BOAs are not the way to increase safety, and that other measures are necessary.Instead of more BOAs, more effective deployment of existing BOAs would be better.

statements.title: Zaanstad should give homeowners a cheap option to buy out their ground lease.
statements.moreInfo: Owner-occupied homes are not always sold with the land underneath them. Sometimes the land is owned by someone else, usually the municipality. People who buy such homes do so under ‘ground lease,’ and must pay an annual fee for use of the land. This annual payment is called the ‘ground rent.’ Homeowners can sometimes buy out their ground rent for a number of years. If they sell their home, the new owner does not need to pay the ground rent until this period expires.
statements.pro: For homeowners, ground lease means financial stress and a lack of security.
statements.con: Ground lease gives the municipality control over what happens to the land.

statements.title: Industry in Zaanstad should expand into areas that are currently green spaces.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad is currently looking to expand industrial zones through development within the existing zones. Examples are HoogTij and the Achtersluispolder.
statements.pro: Industry creates jobs, including for Zaanstad residents.
statements.con: Nature contributes to city liveability, and deserves protection.

statements.title: The municipality should designate as many historical buildings as possible as monuments.
statements.moreInfo: A ‘monument’ is a building, area, or object of historic, architectural, or scientific value that is protected by the government. There are national, provincial, and municipal monuments.  To make almost any kind of change to a monument, the owner requires a permit first.
statements.pro: Heritage is important, because historic buildings embody the history of our region.  Many significant buildings have been demolished in the past: this can be prevented through allocation of monument status.The protection of buildings as historic monuments makes Zaanstad a nicer place to live for residents.
statements.con: Historic buildings can also be preserved without monument status.Official monuments are very costly to the municipality and property owners.Monument status limits the modifications possible to buildings, when they sometimes need to be modified, or demolished to make way for new buildings. To meet modern standards.

statements.title: Zaanstad should install more security cameras in the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad currently has cameras in the nightlife area of Zaandam (including the Dam and the surrounding streets), partly around Zaandam station, and a number of cameras at Krommenie-Assendelft station. The cameras were placed by the municipality to ensure public order and safety. This means that the municipality can use the cameras to monitor activity in those areas. They are not surveillance cameras used by the Public Prosecution Service to detect and investigate criminal offences.
statements.pro: Security cameras increase safety, according to some parties.Security cameras allow criminals to be apprehended, some parties say.
statements.con: Some parties believe that security cameras only provide the illusion of security.Security cameras are an invasion of privacy on Zaanstad residents.

statements.title: The municipality should fund free antenatal classes for Zaanstad residents who are expecting a child.
statements.moreInfo: An antenatal class usually consists of several evening sessions, where expectant parents receive information on pregnancy, childbirth, and parenthood.  Some such courses are covered by supplementary health insurance. The Zaanstad municipal health service (GGD) already offers free antenatal classes for people on minimum wage.
statements.pro: This will allow more expectant parents to prepare properly for the arrival of their child.This helps ensure equal opportunities for all children in Zaanstad.A good start in life is essential for children's healthy development.
statements.con: Free antenatal classes are often already available from the Municipal Public Health Service (GGD).It is reasonable to expect a level of independence from residents, and information on pregnancy and parenthood is already available to expectant parents in Zaanstad.Some parties believe this is unnecessary meddling.

statements.title: The municipality should allow buildings to be demolished to make way for new buildings.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad currently allows buildings to be demolished to make way for new buildings. Exceptions include buildings with monument status, landmark buildings, and village conservation sites.
statements.pro: Demolition is sometimes required for new developments that meet modern standards.Demolishing old buildings and replacing them with new ones allows more homes to be created.
statements.con: Renovation is preferable, in order to preserve landmark buildings.Renovation is cheaper than demolition.

statements.title: At least 40% of new builds in Zaanstad should be social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: Op dit moment is 30% van de huizen in de gemeente Zaanstad een sociale huurwoning. Dat is ongeveer hetzelfde als het landelijke percentage.
statements.pro: What Zaanstad needs is more rental social housing, as the housing shortage is greatest among those seeking this kind of accommodation.According to some parties, building more rental social housing will contribute to neighbourhood diversity.
statements.con: Zaanstad has a relatively high number of residents on low incomes, which puts pressure on services. The problem in the housing market is the lack of mobility. Eliminating the long waiting lists for rental social housing requires more mid-range rentals.

statements.title: The municipality should educate young people on the dangers of owning and using stabbing weapons.
statements.pro: It is important to prevent young people, who are under lots of social pressure, to use knives to commit crime or violence. Education can help, some parties say.
statements.con: Possession of some stabbing weapons is prohibited, and their use for crime or violence is punishable by law. The focus should therefore be on seizing the weapons and punishing perpetrators.

statements.title: People should perform community service or complete study in return for their benefit payments.
statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and receive no other welfare payments can apply for unemployment benefit from the municipality. Some municipalities require that recipients do something in return. In these cases, they perform community service without payment.
statements.pro: This helps people to continue to participate in society and find paid work.Some parties believe it is reasonable to ask something in return for welfare benefits.
statements.con: Not everybody on benefit is able to perform community service or undertake study.Mandatory mutual participation increases stress and distrust.

statements.title: The Zaans Climate Agreement states that the municipality should be climate-neutral by 2040. Zaanstad should try to reach that goal sooner.
statements.moreInfo: An energy-neutral or climate-neutral municipality generates just as much clean energy as residents and businesses consume. The Zaans Climate Agreement has set a target to become climate-neutral between 2030 and 2040. To do so, Zaanstad must reduce its CO2 and other greenhouse-gas emissions that are released through the use of gas, electricity, and fossil fuels. Residents also cause indirect emissions through manufacturing, travel, and homes. Most emissions, however, come from industry. If things continue as they are, Zaanstad estimates its emissions will be halved by 2040. Reaching climate neutrality is a challenge.
statements.pro: The climate crisis means that Zaanstad needs to be climate-neutral as soon as possible.This will enhance residents’ living environment.Some parties believe that a climate-neutral municipality will help residents financially.
statements.con: Zaanstad's contribution to the global climate crisis is negligible.Some parties believe it is not feasible to reach climate neutrality sooner than 2040.Some parties believe it will cost residents more.

statements.title: The Vlinderbrug bridge between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder in Zaandam should be open to cars.
statements.moreInfo: The bridge between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder is meant for public-transport buses. Cyclists and pedestrians can also use it.
statements.pro: Car traffic in Zaandam Zuid will become smoother and faster if cars can use the Vlinderbrug bridge.This will increase safety.
statements.con: This will create more cut-through traffic.The bridge is meant for public transport, bicycles, and pedestrians, which will suffer if they have to share the bridge with cars.

statements.title: Whoever buys a house in our municipality should live in it.
statements.moreInfo: The national government allows municipalities to introduce ‘purchase protection’ for homes up to a certain value.  Anyone who buys such a home must live in it themselves. Leasing the home is only possible in certain situations, such as parents who buy a home for their child. Zaanstad already has purchase protection for homes up to €420,000, which is the national affordability threshold.
statements.pro: According to some parties: homes are for living in, not for making a profit.It creates fairness in the housing market. Home buyers will not need to bid against investors, who drive up the prices.
statements.con: What people do with their houses is their own business, the government has no say in the matter.Buying for rental can help combat the housing shortage, as there is also a shortage of rental housing.

statements.title: Paid parking in Zaanstad should be cheaper.
statements.pro: The municipality should not use paid parking just to make money. Zaanstad should be welcoming to visitors.Parking is a basic necessity.
statements.con: This will increase parking problems. Parking fees in Zaanstad are not high compared to other cities. People should be discouraged from using their cars.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more to combat poverty.
statements.pro: Living in poverty creates health problems and stress.Living in poverty results in debt and social exclusion. The municipality has a responsibility to help the disadvantaged in society.
statements.con: According to some parties, there is no evidence that more funding is necessary to combat poverty.Money should not go directly to poorer residents, it should be spent more effectively. A better way for the municipality to combat poverty would be to invest in education, for example.

statements.title: The ‘caregiver allowance’ for informal carers should be raised from 50 to 140 Euros per year.
statements.moreInfo: Each year, Zaanstad gives informal carers an annual token of appreciation for their efforts. This is called the ‘caregiver allowance.’ Carers aged 18 and over receive €50; those aged between 6 and 17 receive a voucher for €30. To qualify, people must be Zaanstad residents and provide at least 6 hours of care per week within a three-month period, or 250 hours per year, with no financial compensation.
statements.pro: Informal carers are essential in our ageing society, which is why they should be rewarded more.Informal carers save the municipality money, by easing pressure on professional care services.
statements.con: Informal carers do not need money, they need a break. The municipality would be better off investing more in respite care, such as day care programmes, to ease the burden on informal caregivers.An increase from €50 to €140 per year does nothing to show more appreciation for informal carers.

statements.title: The municipality should allow construction on the outskirts of existing villages.
statements.moreInfo: In partnership with the province, the municipality decides on building permits, city expansion, and/or rezoning in the Zaanstad landscape. There is increasing demand for expansion and new housing, including on the outskirts of villages. Zaanstad is committed to building many more homes. But what locations are suitable, and which are absolutely off-limits? How can the unique character of the landscape be preserved? The municipality has some influence on these decisions, but they are also governed by provincial policy, which only permits minor expansion in the valuable provincial countryside.
statements.pro: Zaanstad needs more housing, and space is limited.
statements.con: The green areas around Zaanstad must be protected.

statements.title: Recognized refugees should be given priority for local social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country due to war or persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application, and decides whether they can stay in the Netherlands. If the IND approves, refugees receive residency status. That is why recognized refugees are called ‘status holders’ in Dutch. Every six months, the national government orders municipalities to accommodate a certain number of these refugees. Municipalities are free to decide whether to prioritise them for housing or not. Currently around 8% of rental social housing in Zaanstad goes to recognized refugees.
statements.pro: All vulnerable groups in need of housing should qualify for priority.Some parties say that the number of rental social homes going to recognised refugees in Zaanstad is not very high, and so granting priority is no problem.
statements.con: Recognised refugees should be treated the same as everybody else, which means no priority.Recognised refugees do not deserve priority – young people in Zaanstad do.

statements.title: The Zaan river needs an extra bridge.
statements.moreInfo: There are currently multiple bridges across the Zaan, including the Zaanbrug. The construction of thousands of new homes in places such as Wormer, Wormerveer, and Krommenie is causing a rise in traffic. This is why an additional bridge across the Zaan is being considered, for both Zaanstad and Wormerland. There are no concrete plans as yet, but the bridge would go between Wormer and Wormerveer.
statements.pro: Traffic is becoming congested, an additional bridge is needed.Better traffic flow is safer.
statements.con: We do not need another bridge across the Zaan.We shouldn't be making things any easier for cars in Zaanstad.
